title: Исследование: Кофе полезен для сердца, особенно по утрам
description: Для миллионов людей во всем мире чашка кофе - обязательный утренний ритуал. Теперь ученые все чаще говорят о том, что эта привычка может приносить не только удовольствие, но и реальную пользу для здоровья. Согласно новому исследованию, опубликованному в научном журнале European Heart Journal и цитируемому итальянским новостным агентством Adkronos, кофе может снижать риск сердечно-сосудистых заболеваний, а максимальный эффект наблюдается, если пит

title: Gas price spikes are slamming Senate battleground states

title: Generation Jihad | We didn’t light it, but we tried to fight it
description:
Many Americans believed a conflict with the Islamic Republic would be a new war.
But as Bill Roggio and Behnam Ben Taleblu explain, the truth is simpler: this war began in 1979. Now, the question isn’t how the war started. It’s how it ends. Is this a limited war to degrade the Tehran regime — or the beginning of the end for the Islamic Republic?
The post Generation Jihad | We didnt light it, but we tried to fight it appeared first on FDDs Long Wa

title: HRW urges pressure on Israel over alleged white phosphorus use in Lebanon
description: After accusing Israel of “unlawfully” using white phosphorus over residential areas of a southern Lebanese town last week, Human Rights Watch says Israel “must be pressured by its allied countries” to prevent such incidents from happening again. Speaking to FRANCE 24, HRW Communications Director for the Middle East and North Africa, Ahmed Benchemsi, said countries could “even impose targeted sanctions on officials who are credibly implicated in g

title: Five Iranian women football players granted asylum in Australia
description: Australia on Tuesday granted humanitarian visas to five Iranian women soccer players after they sought asylum, ​fearing persecution on their return home for their refusal to sing the national anthem at an Asia Cup match. "Australians have been moved by the plight of these brave women," Australian Prime Minister Anthony Albanese told a news conference in Canberra  a day after police helped extract the women from their Iranian government handlers.

title: Iran war stifles Hormuz shipping: ‘Unprecedented’ impact on oil & global economy
description: FRANCE 24's François Picard welcomes Jon Marks, author, political scientist and chairman/founder of Cross-border Information. Having followed energy markets for decades, Marks warns that we are in uncharted waters: the current crisis bears little resemblance to any previous shock.

title: Trump says war in Iran will end 'soon' – but threatens to escalate
description: Asked about how long US strikes in #Iran would last for, Donald #Trump assured the #war would end 'very soon'. But soon after, he threatened to escalate the conflict by striking Iran "twenty times harder" if it stopped the oil flow going through the Strait of #Hormuz.

title: Trump says will waive some oil sanctions after talks with Putin
description: US President Donald Trump said Monday that he will waive some sanctions on oil to boost supply and bring down prices, due to market turmoil over war in the Middle East. Trump did not specify any country or provide details on which sanctions would be lifted, but his comments came after speaking to Russia’s Vladimir Putin, a country that has been put under strict oil sanctions by the US and Europe since the invasion of Ukraine.

title: UNESCO sounds alarm over war threat to Middle East's heritage sites
description: UNESCO is sounding the alarm and reiterating its deep concern about the war in the Middle East damaging priceless world heritage sites. Israeli and American bombings are causing daily damage to educational, scientific, and media infrastructure in the region. Some sites have already been damaged.

title: Australia grants asylum to 5 members of Iranian women's football team
description: Australia granted asylum to five members of the Iranian women’s football team who were visiting the country for a tournament when the Iran war began, a government minister said Tuesday. The announcement followed days of urging by Iranian groups in Australia and by US President Donald Trump for the Australian government to help the women, who drew speculation and news coverage in Australia when players didn't sing the Iranian anthem before their f

title: As Iran war rages, how much oil can Gulf states store?
description: With the US and Israel's war on Iran now in its second week, the strain on the Middle East's oil infrastructure couldn't be clearer. Beyond Iranian strikes on pipelines and refineries, the vital Strait of Hormuz waterway is all but shut down, creating backups for twenty million barrels of oil a day, a fifth of the world's supply. And if that oil can't get out, the questions where will it go and for how long will supply be disrupted. France 24’s L

title: 'You can smell the explosives': Inside the Beirut subrubs targeted by Israel
description: Israel has carried out more airstrikes on what it says are Hezbollah targets in the southern suburbs of Beirut. The neighbourhoods there, in the area known as the Dahiyeh, are historically a stronghold of the group. In an unprecedented move, the IDF issued mass evacuation orders for the whole of the southern suburbs and thousands fled the ensuing Israeli air campaign. But far from everyone has left, as France 24’s reporters found, in a rare trip

description: Israel's Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu warned that the country's military offensive against Iran was "not done yet", saying the operation was degrading Iran's clerical leadership, despite comments from US president Donald Trump that the war could be over "very soon". France 24's Olivia Bizot has the latest from Jerusalem.

title: REPLAY: Pentagon chief Hegseth says Iran 'badly losing', strikes to intensify
description: US attacks on Iran will hit a new intensity Tuesday and the war will continue as long as President Donald Trump decides, Pentagon chief Pete Hegseth said. "Today will be yet again, our most intense day of strikes inside Iran -- the most fighters, the most bombers," Hegseth told a news conference at the Pentagon.

title: Europe’s borders under threat from Russia: Baltic states gear up for war
description: On Europe’s eastern flank, the Baltic states – Estonia, Latvia and Lithuania – fear they could become Vladimir Putin’s next target. After several drone incursions from Russia, the three countries are stepping up their defence strategies on the ground and in the air. This report from our colleagues at FRANCE 2 and FRANCE 24’s Guillaume Gougeon.

title: Oil prices fall, stocks rise as Trump says military goals ‘pretty well complete’
description: Global benchmark prices for oil fell, and stocks rose, on the back of US President’s comments signalling the war in Iran could be nearing some form of conclusion. Meanwhile, the Strait of Hormuz is still effectively closed to tanker traffic, and Washington is weighing its options both to clear the bottleneck and ease pressure on consumers back home. Also in this edition, Venezuela prepares to open up its mining sector to US companies.

title: Trump says Iran war could end 'soon', but threatens Iran over oil disruption
description: US President Donald Trump said Monday that the war against Iran could be over "soon", but he left open the possibility of an escalation in fighting if global oil supplies are disrupted by the Islamic Republic. However, in an apparent response to Trump’s remarks, a spokesperson for the paramilitary Revolutionary Guard said “Iran will determine when the war ends.”

title: Oil and stocks whipsaw on hopes of an end to Middle East war

As Gulf countries began cutting production amid a rapid decrease in available storage space, consumers have already started seeing fuel prices rise.

title: Oil and stocks whipsaw on hopes of an end to Iran war

title: Why the Strait of Hormuz is so difficult to defend
description: Map: Sara Wise/AxiosWhile President Trump has offered political risk insurance and Navy escorts for tankers navigating the Strait of Hormuz, it still remains one of the most difficult waterways in the world to defend. The big picture: The Strait, which carries roughly 25% of the world's seaborne oil supply, is approximately 21 nautical miles wide at its narrowest point, but the designated shipping lanes are far smaller — concentrating traffic int

title: France Extends Nuclear Deterrence to European Allies – What Does This Mean for European Security?
description: French President Emmanuel Macron announced the extension of France’s nuclear deterrent to eight European nations, alongside the expansion of France’s nuclear warhead stockpile. In an event held at the ‘Ile Longue’ naval port near Brest, in front of the nuclear armed ballistic missile submarine (SSBN) Le Téméraire, French President Emmanuel Macron announced a significant update []

title: UK Typhoons, Wildcats, and Merlin Deployed to Bolster Counter-Drone Defences
description: The UK has dispatched four more Typhoon FGR4s, two Wildcat HMA2s, and a Merlin HM2 ASaC towards the Middle East to strengthen defences as U.S. strategic bombers begin to use UK bases for operations against Iran. As Type 45 destroyer HMS Dragon prepares to sail for the Eastern Mediterranean, four more Typhoon FGR4s have already []

title: B-1B Bombers Deploy to RAF Fairford to Ramp Up Missions Over Iran
description: Four U.S. Air Force B-1B Lancer bombers arrived at RAF Fairford as the U.S. looks to “surge dramatically” the operations over Iran. After the UK allowed the U.S. to use RAF Fairford and Diego Garcia for the deployment of U.S. Air Force bombers currently involved in operations over Iran, the first four B-1B Lancer bombers []

title: Rockets target US Embassy in Baghdad after Iraqi Shiite militia group issues threat over war in Lebanon
description:
The day before an attack on the US Embassy on March 7, the Coordination Committee of the Iraqi Resistance had issued a statement warning that continued strikes on Beirut’s Dahiyeh area would elicit attacks against embassies and oil companies in the region. The ultimatum follows repeated Israeli strikes against Hezbollah infrastructure and personnel in Beirut’s southern suburbs after Hezbollah entered the war on March 2.
The post Rockets target US

title: Analysis: Similarities and differences in US and Israeli targets and objectives in Iran campaign
description:
The US and Israel have cooperated to achieve impressive operational successes against Iran, but a publicly shared definition of victory remains elusive. This inconsistency and lack of clarity may negatively impact the allies’ ability to achieve durable strategic objectives.
The post Analysis: Similarities and differences in US and Israeli targets and objectives in Iran campaign appeared first on FDDs Long War Journal.

title: Iran and proxy militias strike energy infrastructure, US bases, and Gulf capitals on March 6
description:
On March 6, Iran and its aligned militias continued their campaign of missile and drone attacks across the Middle East, targeting civilian infrastructure, military installations, airports, and political sites in Bahrain, Iraq, Kuwait, Saudi Arabia, Qatar, and the UAE.
The post Iran and proxy militias strike energy infrastructure, US bases, and Gulf capitals on March 6 appeared first on FDDs Long War Journal.

title: Pentagon acknowledges tough quest to counter Iranian drones
description: Pete Hegseth and other military leaders warned lawmakers that gaps in counter-drone technology could leave U.S. forces and assets increasingly vulnerable.

title: US B-1B Lancers arrive at RAF Fairford as strikes on Iran intensify
description: The U.K. Ministry of Defence confirmed Saturday that U.S. forces had begun using the British base for “specific defensive operations."

title: Trump to join grieving families for return of soldiers killed in Iran war
description: The president is joining families at Dover Air Force Base at the dignified transfer for the six U.S. troops killed in the war in the Middle East.

title: Prewar US intel found intervention in Iran wasn’t likely to change leadership
description: A February intelligence assessment determined that American military intervention was not likely to lead to regime change in the Islamic Republic.

title: Bahrain says Iranian drone attack damages water desalination plant
description: Attacks on water systems are uncommon in wartime, but they have appeared in the war in the Middle East with strikes on desalination plants -- critical infrastructure for millions of people in the arid region. Bahrain's interior ministry said Sunday that an Iranian drone attack damaged a water desalination plant, accusing Tehran of "randomly" targeting civilian infrastructure. FRANCE 24's Monte Francis reports.

title: Explosions heard across Doha, sirens sound in Bahrain as Iran strikes Gulf neighbours
description: Several more explosions were heard across Doha and warning sirens sounded in Manama on Monday, as Iran pressed its aerial campaign against Gulf neighbours. The oil-rich Gulf states have been targeted by waves of drones and missiles since Tehran launched its retaliation campaign in the wake of the US and Israeli attacks against Iran. Hoda Abdel-Hamid reports from Doha for FRANCE 24.

title: Macron says France, allies preparing 'defensive' mission to reopen Strait of Hormuz
description: France and its allies are preparing a "defensive" mission to reopen the strategically vital Strait of Hormuz, President Emmanuel Macron said Monday as the Middle East war entered its second week. The French leader landed by helicopter on the Charles de Gaulle aircraft carrier, dispatched to the Mediterranean after US-Israeli strikes on Iran on February 28 triggered a war that has sown chaos in the Middle East and threatens to spill into other reg

title: What to know about Iran’s new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei
description: The son of Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei will now leader Iran through the biggest crisis in the countrys modern history.

title: WHO chief raises alarm after Israeli attacks on Iranian oil facilities
description: Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus says children, the elderly at particular risk after damage to Iranian petroleum facilities.
